urn:contentItem:6JGB-KN63-RX1M-C3D6-00000-00Enphase Energy Unveils Solid-State Transformer for AI Data Centers2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisEnphase Energy Unveils Solid-State Transformer for AI Data Centers

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

30 April 2026 12:15 EDT

FREMONT, Calif. — Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH), a global leader in energy technology, has announced the development of its IQ® Solid-State Transformer (IQ®SST), specifically designed for the evolving needs of AI data centers. The announcement was made on 28th April 2026 at 04:02 PM EDT, marking a significant step forward as the industry shifts towards higher-density DC power architectures.

The IQ SST aims to replace traditional centralized power conversion with a distributed ‘supercluster’ architecture. Each unit will consist of a 1.25 MW rack that incorporates 342 intelligent, semiconductor- and software-defined power modules, achieving an impressive 98.5% efficiency and 99.999% availability through integrated redundancy. This innovative design allows operations to continue seamlessly, even with only 90% of the power modules functioning.

One of the standout features of the IQ SST is its elimination of internal batteries, which reduces or potentially removes the requirement for rack-level battery sidecars and traditional uninterruptible power supply (UPS) systems. For data center operators, this translates into reduced infrastructure costs, less space occupied by electrical equipment, and an increase in available floor space for GPUs. Enphase Energy’s CEO, Badri Kothandaraman, stated, “AI is changing how power must be delivered to compute infrastructure,” underscoring the urgent need for this advanced technology.

The IQ SST is tailored to meet the demands of AI workloads that are driving data center power requirements from hundreds of kilowatts to megawatt-scale densities. With the capability to convert medium-voltage AC directly into regulated DC power in a single conversion stage, the platform supports both 800 VDC and +/-400 VDC configurations, essential for modern AI standards. This approach is expected to provide sub-millisecond response times to fluctuating AI loads, a necessity in today’s fast-paced computing environment.

Enphase Energy plans to manufacture the IQ SST using the same automated platform that has successfully produced approximately 87.8 million microinverters. By sourcing standard high-volume semiconductor components domestically, the company aims to offer an alternative to overseas, custom-built power systems. Looking ahead, Enphase anticipates an annual addressable opportunity in the U.S. for IQ SST in AI data centers to exceed 11 GW by 2031, with customer pilots commencing in 2027 and volume shipments scheduled for 2028.

SECTION 1 ENPHASE ENERGY ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Enphase Energy Activities

Activities

Enphase Energy Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company headquartered in Fremont, California, is a leading innovator in the renewable energy sector. The company specializes in designing, developing, and manufacturing advanced microinverter systems, solar energy storage solutions, and intelligent software platforms that optimize energy production and consumption. At its core, Enphase focuses on enhancing the efficiency and reliability of solar power systems by converting direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) at the module level, reducing risks associated with traditional string inverters. The fastest-growing segments include its IQ-series microinverters and Encharge battery storage systems, which have seen rapid adoption in residential and commercial markets due to increasing demand for grid-independent energy solutions and supportive renewable policies. This growth is fueled by technological advancements, such as integrated app-based monitoring and seamless compatibility with emerging smart home ecosystems, positioning Enphase as a key driver in the global transition to sustainable energy. It is the NASDAQ's 9th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sun May 10, 2026: Enphase Energy (ENPH) Secures $52M Safe Harbor Deal for Domestic Microinverters

Enphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:ENPH) has made a significant move with the signing of a new $52 million safe harbor agreement, aimed at bolstering its domestic operations. On the 7th of May 2026, Enphase Energy entered into this agreement with a US solar and battery financing company that focuses on third-party ownership (TPO) models. The deal will involve the supply of IQ9 Microinverters produced in domestic manufacturing facilities, which will be utilized in upcoming residential and commercial projects.

This agreement is strategically designed to assist project owners in securing eligibility for both the base investment tax credit and the domestic content bonus credit, which are critical incentives for the solar industry. Following this latest contract, Enphase Energy's total physical work test (PWT) order backlog with TPO providers has surged to approximately $873.7 million. Notably, most of this backlog was accumulated earlier in 2026, totaling $754 million prior to this recent agreement.

Enphase Energy anticipates that it will recognize the associated revenue from this contract between 2027 and 2030, depending on specific customer project timelines and prevailing market demand. The current backlog is exclusively comprised of IQ9 Microinverters, indicating potential for additional revenue through the future integration of IQ Batteries, cables, and other accessories, enhancing the overall product offering.

As a leading energy technology company, Enphase Energy specializes in the design and manufacture of microinverter-based solar and battery storage systems tailored for both residential and commercial applications. This positioning allows Enphase Energy to capitalize on the growing demand for renewable energy solutions, particularly as the market shifts towards domestic manufacturing and sustainability.

While Enphase Energy presents a compelling investment opportunity, it is essential to consider the broader landscape of technology stocks. Some analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential with comparatively lower downside risk. Investors are encouraged to explore various sectors to identify undervalued opportunities that may benefit from market trends, including the impact of tariffs and the ongoing trend toward onshoring.

Tue May 5, 2026: Enphase Energy Announces Details for 2026 Annual Stockholder Meeting

Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH), a global energy technology company, has announced that it will hold its Annual Meeting of Stockholders on the 13th of May, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. Pacific Time. The meeting is set to take place at the company's headquarters located at 47281 Bayside Parkway, Fremont, California.

The announcement, made on 5th May 2026, includes details about the voting process and participation for stockholders, which can be found in the company's 2026 Proxy Statement filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. This meeting serves as a significant platform for Enphase Energy to connect with its investors and discuss key business developments.

Following the formal portion of the Annual Meeting, Enphase Energy's President and CEO Badri Kothandaraman will deliver a presentation at approximately 9:30 a.m. Pacific Time. The presentation will be accessible via the internet and telephone, with registration required for online attendance. This part of the event is expected to provide valuable insights into the company's strategic direction and performance.

Enphase Energy, recognized as a leader in microinverter-based solar and battery systems, has made significant strides in the renewable energy sector. With over 5.2 million Enphase-based systems deployed in more than 165 countries, the company continues to expand its footprint in the energy technology landscape. Investors are particularly keen on updates regarding new product developments and market expansions that may be discussed during the meeting.

For further information about Enphase Energy's 2026 Annual Meeting and CEO Presentation, stakeholders are encouraged to visit the company's Investor Relations website. As the company gears up for this important event, market watchers will be keen to assess how Enphase Energy plans to navigate the evolving energy landscape and maintain its competitive edge.

Mon May 4, 2026: Enphase Energy Unveils White Paper on IQ SST Architecture for AI Data Centers

Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) has published a technical white paper titled "IQ Solid-State Transformer: Intelligent Power for AI," aimed at explaining the intricacies of its IQ Solid-State Transformer (IQSST) architecture. The document provides an in-depth engineering perspective on how Enphase's distributed supercluster architecture, custom ASIC predictive control, and GaN-based power conversion are engineered to facilitate rapid responses for 800 VDC (+/-400 VDC) artificial intelligence infrastructure. This publication was made available on the Enphase website on the 4th of May, 2026.

The AI data center sector is transitioning towards higher-voltage direct current (DC) frameworks to accommodate the increasing demands of rack power densities. The white paper addresses the challenges associated with this shift, particularly the need for efficient conversion of medium-voltage alternating current (AC) to regulated DC power, which necessitates rapid transient response, high availability, and scalability. Enphase's IQ SST aims to solve these challenges with an innovative approach that utilizes a distributed supercluster of 342 smaller power modules, functioning as a cohesive system, rather than relying on larger monolithic SST designs.

Enphase's strategy involves leveraging Kestrel, its custom control ASIC, which is tailored to provide the predictive control essential for this single-stage power conversion architecture. Furthermore, GaN-based switching technology is expected to enhance power efficiency during high-frequency operations, while the proprietary high-frequency transformer aims to minimize electromagnetic interference (EMI). Together, these advancements are designed to enable reduced local energy buffering and may even eliminate the need for sidecar power racks in certain data center configurations.

Raghu Belur, co-founder and chief product officer at Enphase Energy, emphasized the importance of their architectural choice to utilize numerous smaller power modules instead of a few large units. This design philosophy underpins the intended performance metrics of the IQ SST, including redundancy, serviceability, and manufacturability. The white paper delineates several key technical differentiators, including single-stage power conversion, rapid response to dynamic AI loads, high system availability through parallel redundancy, and scalable manufacturing capabilities that align with Enphase's existing production foundation.

In summary, the white paper not only showcases the IQ SST's advanced architecture but also marks a significant step in Enphase Energy's two-decade journey in distributed power electronics, now extending into the realm of AI-scale power infrastructure. As the demand for efficient and reliable power solutions in AI data centers continues to grow, Enphase Energy's innovations may play a pivotal role in shaping the future of this sector.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Enphase Energy Partners with Evergen to Enhance Australian Home Battery Systems

Enphase Energy has unveiled a strategic integration between its IQ Batteries and Evergen, a prominent distributed energy and virtual power plant (VPP) orchestration platform utilized by various major energy retailers across Australia and New Zealand. This collaboration is set to enhance how homeowners effectively manage and capitalize on their solar and battery systems. Notably, on 27th April 2026, Enphase, recognized globally for its microinverter-based solar and battery solutions, announced that it supports over 65 VPP programs across eight countries, reinforcing its commitment to empower households to engage actively in grid services, thereby boosting energy flexibility and system efficiency.

Evergen's sophisticated optimization and VPP platform enables energy retailers to provide Enphase system owners with smarter energy management options. Homeowners can now charge their batteries during low electricity pricing periods, export stored energy back to the grid during peak pricing times, and participate in grid-support programs that financially reward users for flexible energy consumption. The availability of these features is contingent upon factors such as the retailer, system configuration, and local state regulations. Eligible homeowners may also take advantage of various financial incentives, including state-level programs in regions like South Australia and New South Wales, retailer sign-up bonuses, and ongoing participation credits.

Moreover, Australia's federal Cheaper Home Batteries Program offers a potential reduction of up to 30% on the upfront cost of eligible battery systems, subject to specific terms and conditions. Evergen states that this integration enhances both choice and value for Australian households, enabling them to optimize their Enphase battery systems, engage in VPP programs, and create new income opportunities while contributing to a more stable and resilient electricity grid. The confluence of federal rebates, state incentives, and retailer-driven programs is fostering robust momentum for home battery system adoption.

Through effective collaborations with platforms like Evergen and additional integrations such as Amber Electric, Enphase Energy customers are equipped with enhanced tools to minimize costs and maximize the value derived from their energy systems. Enphase emphasized that Australia remains a global leader in home energy innovation and views this integration as a pivotal move toward empowering homeowners with greater control over their energy usage, ultimately improving the return on their solar and battery investments. By merging financial incentives, advanced optimization platforms, and cutting-edge technology, this initiative is expected to accelerate the adoption of battery systems and provide a more efficient and flexible energy experience for consumers.

As Enphase Energy continues to push the boundaries of renewable energy solutions, the integration with Evergen signifies a significant step forward in the evolution of energy management for Australian homeowners. With the promise of smarter energy utilization and increased financial rewards, this partnership is anticipated to reshape the energy landscape, making it not only more efficient but also more economically viable for consumers.

May 11, 2026: SoftBank's Plan to Supply Residential Markets with Zinc-Halogen Batteries Introduces Long-Term Competitive Consideration for Enphase Energy

SoftBank Corp., the mobile unit of SoftBank Group, announced on May 11, 2026, that it will commence large-scale battery cell manufacturing at its Sakai, Osaka plant to power its AI data centers. According to industry reports, the company will partner with South Korea's Cosmos Lab, which contributes zinc-halogen chemistry using pure water as an electrolyte to eliminate fire risk, and DeltaX Co., which provides high energy-density designs.

SoftBank plans to begin manufacturing in FY2027, scale to one gigawatt-hour annually by FY2028, ranking among Japan's largest facilities according to industry analysis, and ramp toward several GWh by approximately 2031. Batteries will initially power AI data centers, then expand into grid, factory, industrial, and residential markets globally. SoftBank targets revenue exceeding 100 billion yen for its domestic battery business by FY2030.

Impact on Enphase Energy: ENPH

Following this announcement, Enphase Energy faces a longer-term competitive consideration as SoftBank has explicitly stated it will eventually supply its zinc-halogen batteries for residential markets globally. While SoftBank's near-term focus is AI data centers and grid applications, the medium-term residential expansion targets the same end market where Enphase has been building its IQ Battery franchise.

Zinc-halogen's non-flammable characteristic could serve as a marketing differentiator in residential markets where lithium-ion fire-safety concerns occasionally surface. Enphase's installer network, microinverter integration, and home energy management software remain durable competitive advantages.

Apr 16, 2026: China Considers Solar Manufacturing Equipment Export Curbs Targeting U.S. Market, Posing Supply Chain Risk to Installer-Dependent Business Models

Chinese authorities have initiated consultations with solar panel equipment providers regarding potential limits on exports of advanced manufacturing technology to the United States, according to five people with knowledge of the discussions. China accounts for an estimated 80% of global solar panel component production and is home to the world's top ten suppliers of solar cell manufacturing equipment. Discussions with one equipment manufacturer focused on possible restrictions covering high-end machinery required for producing higher-efficiency panels using HJT technology. No formal rule has been finalized, and the consultations have not progressed to the stage of soliciting official industry feedback.

The prospective measure would extend export controls into an additional technology sector where China holds a dominant position, building on earlier restrictions applied to rare earth exports. The deliberations are taking place ahead of a planned bilateral summit the following month. The United States remains structurally dependent on imports for upstream solar components, with domestic solar cell manufacturing capacity of approximately 3.2 gigawatts against roughly 60 gigawatts of module production capacity, highlighting a structural gap in domestic supply that these restrictions could make harder to close.

If adopted, restrictions on advanced HJT manufacturing equipment could constrain the ability of U.S. solar manufacturers to scale domestic cell production, putting upward pressure on panel costs and potentially dampening installation growth across the residential and commercial solar markets.

Impact on Enphase Energy (ENPH)

Enphase Energy faces indirect but meaningful operational risk tied to a potential slowdown in U.S. solar panel manufacturing capacity expansion. Enphase's core business, encompassing microinverters and energy management systems, depends on robust residential and commercial solar installation activity, which in turn requires consistent access to competitively priced solar panels.

If China's potential export curbs constrain U.S. manufacturers' ability to scale domestic solar cell production using advanced HJT equipment, panel supply constraints or cost increases could dampen installation growth, reducing demand for Enphase's microinverters and storage systems. The domestic solar cell manufacturing capacity gap, at approximately 3.2 gigawatts against roughly 60 gigawatts of module production capacity, underscores the structural vulnerability of U.S. solar supply chains to upstream equipment restrictions.

Enphase's ongoing expansion into battery storage and virtual power plant services provides some degree of business diversification, but solar installation volume remains the primary driver of its addressable market growth in the United States.

May 10 2026: Enphase Energy (ENPH) Secures $52M Safe Harbor Deal for Domestic Microinverters

Enphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:ENPH) has made a significant move with the signing of a new $52 million safe harbor agreement, aimed at bolstering its domestic operations. On the 7th of May 2026, Enphase Energy entered into this agreement with a US solar and battery financing company that focuses on third-party ownership (TPO) models. The deal will involve the supply of IQ9 Microinverters produced in domestic manufacturing facilities, which will be utilized in upcoming residential and commercial projects.

This agreement is strategically designed to assist project owners in securing eligibility for both the base investment tax credit and the domestic content bonus credit, which are critical incentives for the solar industry. Following this latest contract, Enphase Energy's total physical work test (PWT) order backlog with TPO providers has surged to approximately $873.7 million. Notably, most of this backlog was accumulated earlier in 2026, totaling $754 million prior to this recent agreement.

Enphase Energy anticipates that it will recognize the associated revenue from this contract between 2027 and 2030, depending on specific customer project timelines and prevailing market demand. The current backlog is exclusively comprised of IQ9 Microinverters, indicating potential for additional revenue through the future integration of IQ Batteries, cables, and other accessories, enhancing the overall product offering.

As a leading energy technology company, Enphase Energy specializes in the design and manufacture of microinverter-based solar and battery storage systems tailored for both residential and commercial applications. This positioning allows Enphase Energy to capitalize on the growing demand for renewable energy solutions, particularly as the market shifts towards domestic manufacturing and sustainability.

While Enphase Energy presents a compelling investment opportunity, it is essential to consider the broader landscape of technology stocks. Some analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential with comparatively lower downside risk. Investors are encouraged to explore various sectors to identify undervalued opportunities that may benefit from market trends, including the impact of tariffs and the ongoing trend toward onshoring.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-N6W3-RYBM-N04R-00000-00Geothermal Developer Fervo Energy Raises $1.9 Billion in Upsized IPO2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS, IPO news & STOCKWATCHGeothermal Developer Fervo Energy Raises $1.9 Billion in Upsized IPOMark Segal

Link to Image

Geothermal energy project developer Fervo Energy announced the finalized pricing of its initial public offering, with the company selling 70 million shares at $27, following strong demand which saw the IPO upsized from an initial planned 55.6 million shares at an expected price of $21 to $24. The IPO values the company at approximately $7.7 billion.

The IPO comes as the U.S. power market faces surging clean energy demand driven by factors including a rapid buildout of power-hungry AI infrastructure and a shift to electrification. In a letter included in the company's SEC IPO filing documents, Fervo Co-Founder and CEO Tim Latimer wrote:

"American economic prosperity has always depended on affordable, reliable energy. And as the U.S. confronts a global AI race, and increasing geopolitical and environmental uncertainty, this fundamental truth will only deepen.  There is no AI revolution without power for data centers. There is no industrial renaissance without power for manufacturing facilities and military bases. There is no future where we address the emissions causing climate change without power for mass electrification."

Founded in 2017, Houston, Texas-based Fervo Energy focuses on the development of enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) projects to deliver carbon-free energy. The company uses techniques utilized by the oil and gas industry such as horizontal drilling and distributed fiber optic sensing to transform reservoirs of hot rock beneath the earth's surface into clean energy sources.

The company has signed a series of power purchase agreements with hyperscalers, including Google, and with utilities and energy companies including Southern California Edison, NV Energy, and Shell, with over 658 MW in contracted offtake deals to date.

Fervo is currently developing its first greenfield development, Cape Station, in Beaver County, Utah. The project produces energy by injecting water into hot subsurface rock formations, extracting the heated water, and using it to generate electricity. Cape Station is expected to begin delivering first power to the grid in 2026, reaching approximately 100 MW of capacity by early 2027, with plans to scale to 500 MW.

In the letter, Latimer added:

"Today, miles-long lines for gasoline have been replaced by lines for electricity. Tech companies compete for megawatts to claim AI market share. Manufacturers jockey for power to strengthen American industry. Utilities demand clean, firm electricity to stabilize the grid. Fervo is prepared to serve all of these customers. Not with complex, idiosyncratic projects but with a simplified, standardized product capable of delivering around-the-clock, carbon-free power using proven oil and gas technology."

value.Title: Week in review Some iPhone owners could get up to $95 from settlement

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ7-VMP3-RTCD-P0Y9-00000-00Week in review2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisBUSINESSPg. 11Week in reviewSome iPhone owners could get up to $95 from settlement

Owners of some iPhones are in line to get cash payments of up to $95 from Apple after the company on Tuesday reached a $250 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit for false advertising of its artificial intelligence capabilities.

Apple trumpeted new AI features for its virtual assistant Siri when it rolled out the iPhone 16 in 2024, part of new software updates that the company billed as “Apple Intelligence.”

The company has been scrambling to keep up with tech rivals amid the AI boom but still hasn’t delivered on the Siri revamp two years later.

The lawsuit, filed on behalf of U.S. consumers in the San Franciscofederal court for the Northern District of California, alleged that Apple deceived consumers with a marketing campaign that promoted features that did not yet exist and misled them into buying the devices.

Lawyers for the iPhone buyers asked a court for preliminary approval of the proposed $250 million settlement, according to a court filling. If approved by a judge, it would be one of the biggest ever for Apple.

The settlement covers about 37 million devices bought in the U.S. from June 10, 2024, to March 29, 2025, including all iPhone 16 models and the iPhone 15 Pro and iPhone 15 Pro Max.

State probing Trump deal on wind project

California is investigating one of the Donald Trump administration’s deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California’s central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company’s recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

Under the first deal, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

Fed sees risks rising from inflation

Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem said there is much uncertainty over the future path for the economy and monetary policy, but he sees risks rising more for inflation than for employment.

“Inflation is running meaningfully above our target of 2%,” Musalem said Wednesday during an event in Fairhope, Alabama, organized by the Mississippi Bankers Association. “We have risks both on the employment side and on the inflation side. In my understanding, the risks have been shifting towards more risk on the inflation side than the employment side.”

Musalem said the Fed’s benchmark policy rate is either at the neutral level that neither boosts nor slows the economy, but it might also be slightly accommodative.

“There are very plausible scenarios under which the economy would require us to keep the policy rate at its current level for some time,” he said.

He also noted he sees scenarios under which officials may need to lower rates further or raise rates.

“So a lot of uncertainty right now, and it’s important to see how things settle,” Musalem said.

Compiled from Associated Press and Bloomberg reports.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-2XJ3-S207-D27H-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON CHOSEN FOR GROUNDBREAKING FIRE DEPARTMENT TRAINING FACILITY

Swinerton Builders has been selected as the Construction Manager/General Contractor for a new $145 million training facility for the San Francisco Fire Department (SFFD). This significant contract, awarded by San Francisco Public Works, is aimed at enhancing public safety and emergency response capabilities in the Bay Area. The groundbreaking for the facility took place on 16th April 2026, marking a pivotal step in modernizing the training infrastructure for the city's first responders.Located in the southeastern district of San Francisco, the new SFFD Division of Training campus will feature a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to replicate the unique characteristics of San Francisco neighborhoods. The facility will include various training environments, such as sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and structures that simulate post-earthquake conditions, enabling recruits and veterans alike to experience realistic emergency scenarios. Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager of Swinerton's San Francisco/East Bay division, emphasized the importance of this center in equipping first responders with essential training resources.In addition to the scenario district, the state-of-the-art center will host emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and community-based Neighborhood Emergency Response Team (NERT) training sessions. The Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building, ensuring immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and serving as a backup emergency operations center. This multi-faceted approach reflects Swinerton's commitment to enhancing community resilience and preparedness.Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, highlighted the collaborative nature of the project, noting the company's extensive experience with over 250 projects in San Francisco, including more than 40 public safety facilities. She reaffirmed Swinerton's dedication to working closely with city stakeholders and community members to deliver a facility tailored to the needs of San Francisco's emergency services. The company's hands-on approach will involve its skilled craft employees executing key construction elements, ensuring high standards of quality and safety.With a legacy spanning over 135 years, Swinerton Builders is well-positioned to lead this transformative project. Their expertise in building public service infrastructure underscores a deep commitment to safety, innovation, and collaboration. As the construction of the SFFD Division of Training facility progresses, it promises to serve as a critical asset to the San Francisco Fire Department, enhancing the city's readiness to respond to emergencies and safeguarding the community for years to come.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. The development of this tool involved collaboration with over 80 stakeholders through design exercises and field pilots, ensuring its relevance and effectiveness.Since its pilot introduction, SafeT Coach has recorded more than 25,000 interactions involving Turner staff, trade partners, and field teams. For instance, Darren Dreas, a superintendent working on a higher-education lab project, utilized the tool to evaluate whether a vertical shaft met the criteria for a permit-required confined space. With SafeT Coach, he was able to quickly access a decision flow chart, a start-of-day permit checklist, and relevant policy citations, facilitating an informed discussion with a trade partner’s safety manager.Dreas commented on the efficiency of the tool, stating, “Using SafeT Coach saved hours of research and analysis, helping us to quickly make informed decisions.” This positive feedback underscores the potential impact of SafeT Coach on improving safety protocols in the construction industry, enabling better decision-making on-site and promoting a culture of safety.As construction continues to evolve with technology integration, Turner Construction's introduction of SafeT Coach represents a significant step towards leveraging AI for practical safety solutions. The tool's free accessibility opens opportunities for various companies across the industry to enhance their safety measures without incurring additional costs, potentially leading to broader adoption of advanced safety practices.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR MAJOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT PROJECT

Turner Construction Company has been selected to serve as the general contractor for the ambitious Westcourt project, a $500 million mixed-use development located adjacent to the Kia Center in downtown Orlando. This announcement was made on 5th May 2026, marking a significant step forward for a project that has faced multiple delays in recent years.The Westcourt development is planned to cover approximately 8.5 acres and will feature a sports and entertainment district ranging from 900,000 to 1 million square feet. Key components of the project include a 265-key Kimpton-branded hotel, 269 high-rise residential units, and up to 310,000 square feet of Class A office space. Additionally, the plan encompasses over 125,000 square feet dedicated to dining and entertainment, a 3,500-capacity venue for live events, and 1,140 on-site parking spaces.Turner Construction has indicated that during peak construction periods, the project will support around 800 workers on-site and is expected to create approximately 1,600 direct construction jobs throughout its duration. The firm anticipates engaging many trade partners from the Central Florida area, which could bolster local employment opportunities.In April 2024, the project received necessary land use entitlements, with the Kimpton hotel partnership announced later that same year, marking the brand's inaugural location in Orlando. Public support has also been secured, with Orlando’s Community Redevelopment Agency committing $2.5 million to the project, potentially reimbursing the developer up to $40 million over time through future property tax growth generated by Westcourt.Looking ahead, development documents suggest a targeted groundbreaking in the second quarter of 2026, with the projected opening set for late 2028. While construction cranes are not expected to rise immediately, the attachment of a reputable builder like Turner Construction brings renewed momentum to one of downtown Orlando's most anticipated projects.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INDIAN OPERATIONS WITH NEW WEWORK WORKSPACE

Turner Construction is solidifying its presence in India with the establishment of a new managed workspace in Airoli, Mumbai. This expansion marks a significant milestone for the global construction firm as it partners with WeWork India. The new facility, which spans 46,000 square feet and accommodates over 650 professionals, was opened on the 7th of May, 2026, reflecting Turner’s long-term commitment to India's infrastructure and talent ecosystem.The decision by Turner Construction to consolidate its teams in a large-scale managed office underscores the growing recognition among enterprises that workspace design can be a strategic enabler of collaboration and operational agility. Exclusively designed for Turner, this office aligns with the company's global standards while leveraging WeWork India's design capabilities. Jairam Panch, Chief Operating Officer of Turner Construction, stated that the new workspace is more than just a physical location; it embodies the company's investment in an environment that fosters collaboration, creativity, and excellence.Turner’s relationship with WeWork India began in 2022 with a modest 16-seat private office, which has now evolved into a comprehensive enterprise-grade managed office solution. As Turner's operations in India expanded, the need for a flexible workspace that supports employee convenience and corporate identity became paramount. Airoli was chosen for its connectivity, talent access, and burgeoning business ecosystem. The managed office incorporates Turner’s precision-focused approach along with WeWork India's design thinking, featuring collaborative spaces and modern amenities.The rise in demand for managed offices like the one established by Turner Construction reflects a broader shift in enterprise perspectives on workspaces. As noted by Santosh Martin, Chief Revenue Officer of WeWork India, companies are increasingly viewing their work environments not just as operational assets, but as critical drivers of organizational performance and culture. Turner’s transition from a small office to a larger, customized workspace exemplifies the trend towards flexible models that facilitate growth while maintaining a strong corporate identity.With this latest addition, WeWork India continues to expand its footprint among enterprise clients, with 76 percent of its member base in Mumbai representing various sectors, including technology and finance. Operating across eight cities with over 100,000 desks, WeWork India is positioning flexible workspaces as an essential component of modern business infrastructure in the country.

+ COLLABORATIVE STRUCTURES DOMINATES OGCA AWARDS; PCL'S THREE WINS HIGHLIGHT INDUSTRY LEADERSHIP

Collaborative Structures Limited was celebrated for its exceptional contributions to Ontario's construction sector, winning four prestigious awards at the Ontario General Contractors Association (OGCA) Builder Awards. The event took place on the 17th of April, 2026, and recognized exemplary projects including the AIM Recycling facility in Kitchener and the renovation of Conestoga College's Indigenous Classrooms.PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. also secured notable recognition during the awards ceremony, earning accolades for three significant projects: the second phase of the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination, the Rogers Centre Renovation, and Limberlost Place. These achievements underscore PCL's commitment to delivering high-quality infrastructure and innovative solutions across Ontario.The OGCA Builder Awards honor projects that exemplify innovation, safety performance, and overall excellence in construction. Projects recognized this year ranged in value from $5 million to over $300 million, showcasing outstanding achievements across various sectors, including buildings, infrastructure, and renovation, as stated by the OGCA via social media.PCL's specific awards included the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination Phase 2, which involved a project value between $150 million and $300 million. This renovation project, commissioned by Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment (MLSE), aims to modernize and enhance fan experiences, completing its second phase in April 2025. Additionally, the Rogers Centre Renovation, valued at over $300 million, focused on redesigning the seating bowl and player facilities, demonstrating PCL's capability in executing complex renovations within operational venues.The Limberlost Place project also garnered attention for its role in advancing sustainable construction practices. As Ontario's first institutional mass timber building, it represents a significant step in green building innovation. PCL collaborated with George Brown Polytechnic and other partners to educate on mass timber construction, further solidifying its role as a leader in this emerging field. Geoff Howe, senior vice president at PCL, emphasized the company's commitment to realizing client visions while navigating unique project challenges, reflecting the breadth of experience PCL brings to Ontario's construction landscape.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES SPOT ON FORTUNE 100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been recognized as one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2026. This acknowledgment comes as a testament to PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace environment, reflecting the company's dedication to employee satisfaction and engagement. The official announcement was made on 2nd April 2026.This marks the 17th time PCL Construction has earned a place on this prestigious list, achieving a notable rank of 91 this year. The recognition highlights PCL's sustained efforts in creating a supportive and empowering work environment, where employees feel valued and motivated to contribute to the company's success.Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of PCL's U.S. operations, emphasized that the company's employee-owned structure plays a critical role in shaping its culture. He noted, 'Our people are directly invested in shaping a culture where they feel trusted, supported and excited about the future.' This approach not only enhances employee morale but also drives the overall performance of PCL Construction.PCL's recognition comes on the heels of a successful year, during which the company also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 Contractors list for 2025. This accolade underscores PCL Construction's leadership in executing complex projects across North America while maintaining its focus on employee well-being and satisfaction.The Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience. Companies like PCL Construction, which consistently rank high on this list, often see a positive correlation between employee trust and business performance, reinforcing the belief that a supportive workplace is essential for long-term success.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. + MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. urn:contentItem:6JJW-3NN3-RSP0-R4M5-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON CHOSEN FOR GROUNDBREAKING FIRE DEPARTMENT TRAINING FACILITY

Swinerton Builders has been selected as the Construction Manager/General Contractor for a new $145 million training facility for the San Francisco Fire Department (SFFD). This significant contract, awarded by San Francisco Public Works, is aimed at enhancing public safety and emergency response capabilities in the Bay Area. The groundbreaking for the facility took place on 16th April 2026, marking a pivotal step in modernizing the training infrastructure for the city's first responders.Located in the southeastern district of San Francisco, the new SFFD Division of Training campus will feature a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to replicate the unique characteristics of San Francisco neighborhoods. The facility will include various training environments, such as sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and structures that simulate post-earthquake conditions, enabling recruits and veterans alike to experience realistic emergency scenarios. Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager of Swinerton's San Francisco/East Bay division, emphasized the importance of this center in equipping first responders with essential training resources.In addition to the scenario district, the state-of-the-art center will host emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and community-based Neighborhood Emergency Response Team (NERT) training sessions. The Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building, ensuring immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and serving as a backup emergency operations center. This multi-faceted approach reflects Swinerton's commitment to enhancing community resilience and preparedness.Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, highlighted the collaborative nature of the project, noting the company's extensive experience with over 250 projects in San Francisco, including more than 40 public safety facilities. She reaffirmed Swinerton's dedication to working closely with city stakeholders and community members to deliver a facility tailored to the needs of San Francisco's emergency services. The company's hands-on approach will involve its skilled craft employees executing key construction elements, ensuring high standards of quality and safety.With a legacy spanning over 135 years, Swinerton Builders is well-positioned to lead this transformative project. Their expertise in building public service infrastructure underscores a deep commitment to safety, innovation, and collaboration. As the construction of the SFFD Division of Training facility progresses, it promises to serve as a critical asset to the San Francisco Fire Department, enhancing the city's readiness to respond to emergencies and safeguarding the community for years to come.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, emphasized that Quackenbush's leadership will enhance the team's capacity to meet client needs, ranging from facility management to large-scale ground-up projects. Furthermore, Quackenbush is actively involved in community engagement, serving on several boards and partnering with local nonprofits.Nicholas Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, adds more than 20 years of international business development experience to the team. His background includes managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, as well as founding a design-build firm in San Francisco. Quackenbush noted Schifferle's ability to adapt to rapid industry changes and his people-focused approach, which has made him a valuable asset to both the team and its clients. Schifferle's longstanding involvement with CoreNet Global reflects his commitment to advancing the commercial real estate sector.Meanwhile, Robert Murphy, with his extensive portfolio of overseeing over $650 million in commercial interiors construction, is tasked with leading multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. Quackenbush praised Murphy's operational capabilities and his ability to elevate team performance. Together with long-standing division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana, Murphy will work to grow Swinerton’s footprint in the Northeast's commercial interiors market.Since its establishment in the Northeast in 2021, Swinerton's division has delivered a variety of significant projects, including office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up constructions across all five boroughs. With a robust project pipeline and a commitment to excellence, the team continues to expand its portfolio into education, residential, and public sectors. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ COLLABORATIVE STRUCTURES DOMINATES OGCA AWARDS; PCL'S THREE WINS HIGHLIGHT INDUSTRY LEADERSHIP

Collaborative Structures Limited was celebrated for its exceptional contributions to Ontario's construction sector, winning four prestigious awards at the Ontario General Contractors Association (OGCA) Builder Awards. The event took place on the 17th of April, 2026, and recognized exemplary projects including the AIM Recycling facility in Kitchener and the renovation of Conestoga College's Indigenous Classrooms.PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. also secured notable recognition during the awards ceremony, earning accolades for three significant projects: the second phase of the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination, the Rogers Centre Renovation, and Limberlost Place. These achievements underscore PCL's commitment to delivering high-quality infrastructure and innovative solutions across Ontario.The OGCA Builder Awards honor projects that exemplify innovation, safety performance, and overall excellence in construction. Projects recognized this year ranged in value from $5 million to over $300 million, showcasing outstanding achievements across various sectors, including buildings, infrastructure, and renovation, as stated by the OGCA via social media.PCL's specific awards included the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination Phase 2, which involved a project value between $150 million and $300 million. This renovation project, commissioned by Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment (MLSE), aims to modernize and enhance fan experiences, completing its second phase in April 2025. Additionally, the Rogers Centre Renovation, valued at over $300 million, focused on redesigning the seating bowl and player facilities, demonstrating PCL's capability in executing complex renovations within operational venues.The Limberlost Place project also garnered attention for its role in advancing sustainable construction practices. As Ontario's first institutional mass timber building, it represents a significant step in green building innovation. PCL collaborated with George Brown Polytechnic and other partners to educate on mass timber construction, further solidifying its role as a leader in this emerging field. Geoff Howe, senior vice president at PCL, emphasized the company's commitment to realizing client visions while navigating unique project challenges, reflecting the breadth of experience PCL brings to Ontario's construction landscape.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES SPOT ON FORTUNE 100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been recognized as one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2026. This acknowledgment comes as a testament to PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace environment, reflecting the company's dedication to employee satisfaction and engagement. The official announcement was made on 2nd April 2026.This marks the 17th time PCL Construction has earned a place on this prestigious list, achieving a notable rank of 91 this year. The recognition highlights PCL's sustained efforts in creating a supportive and empowering work environment, where employees feel valued and motivated to contribute to the company's success.Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of PCL's U.S. operations, emphasized that the company's employee-owned structure plays a critical role in shaping its culture. He noted, 'Our people are directly invested in shaping a culture where they feel trusted, supported and excited about the future.' This approach not only enhances employee morale but also drives the overall performance of PCL Construction.PCL's recognition comes on the heels of a successful year, during which the company also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 Contractors list for 2025. This accolade underscores PCL Construction's leadership in executing complex projects across North America while maintaining its focus on employee well-being and satisfaction.The Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience. Companies like PCL Construction, which consistently rank high on this list, often see a positive correlation between employee trust and business performance, reinforcing the belief that a supportive workplace is essential for long-term success.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.
