urn:contentItem:6JJV-8W83-RXDC-D1DR-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON CHOSEN FOR GROUNDBREAKING FIRE DEPARTMENT TRAINING FACILITY

Swinerton Builders has been selected as the Construction Manager/General Contractor for a new $145 million training facility for the San Francisco Fire Department (SFFD). This significant contract, awarded by San Francisco Public Works, is aimed at enhancing public safety and emergency response capabilities in the Bay Area. The groundbreaking for the facility took place on 16th April 2026, marking a pivotal step in modernizing the training infrastructure for the city's first responders.Located in the southeastern district of San Francisco, the new SFFD Division of Training campus will feature a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to replicate the unique characteristics of San Francisco neighborhoods. The facility will include various training environments, such as sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and structures that simulate post-earthquake conditions, enabling recruits and veterans alike to experience realistic emergency scenarios. Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager of Swinerton's San Francisco/East Bay division, emphasized the importance of this center in equipping first responders with essential training resources.In addition to the scenario district, the state-of-the-art center will host emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and community-based Neighborhood Emergency Response Team (NERT) training sessions. The Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building, ensuring immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and serving as a backup emergency operations center. This multi-faceted approach reflects Swinerton's commitment to enhancing community resilience and preparedness.Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, highlighted the collaborative nature of the project, noting the company's extensive experience with over 250 projects in San Francisco, including more than 40 public safety facilities. She reaffirmed Swinerton's dedication to working closely with city stakeholders and community members to deliver a facility tailored to the needs of San Francisco's emergency services. The company's hands-on approach will involve its skilled craft employees executing key construction elements, ensuring high standards of quality and safety.With a legacy spanning over 135 years, Swinerton Builders is well-positioned to lead this transformative project. Their expertise in building public service infrastructure underscores a deep commitment to safety, innovation, and collaboration. As the construction of the SFFD Division of Training facility progresses, it promises to serve as a critical asset to the San Francisco Fire Department, enhancing the city's readiness to respond to emergencies and safeguarding the community for years to come.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, emphasized that Quackenbush's leadership will enhance the team's capacity to meet client needs, ranging from facility management to large-scale ground-up projects. Furthermore, Quackenbush is actively involved in community engagement, serving on several boards and partnering with local nonprofits.Nicholas Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, adds more than 20 years of international business development experience to the team. His background includes managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, as well as founding a design-build firm in San Francisco. Quackenbush noted Schifferle's ability to adapt to rapid industry changes and his people-focused approach, which has made him a valuable asset to both the team and its clients. Schifferle's longstanding involvement with CoreNet Global reflects his commitment to advancing the commercial real estate sector.Meanwhile, Robert Murphy, with his extensive portfolio of overseeing over $650 million in commercial interiors construction, is tasked with leading multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. Quackenbush praised Murphy's operational capabilities and his ability to elevate team performance. Together with long-standing division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana, Murphy will work to grow Swinerton’s footprint in the Northeast's commercial interiors market.Since its establishment in the Northeast in 2021, Swinerton's division has delivered a variety of significant projects, including office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up constructions across all five boroughs. With a robust project pipeline and a commitment to excellence, the team continues to expand its portfolio into education, residential, and public sectors. Quackenbush affirmed that the division is well-positioned for future growth, driven by a talented team dedicated to serving the communities in which they operate.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. His ability to navigate complex projects while fostering teamwork aligns with the company's strategic goals.As Hensel Phelps embarks on new initiatives, Choy's leadership will likely contribute to the successful execution of high-profile projects. Stakeholders and investors will be watching closely as the company continues to build its reputation in the construction sector.

+ TRACK3D PARTNERS WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR MAJOR CONSTRUCTION ROLLOUT

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has announced a significant milestone in its growth trajectory as it secures a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps. As of 25th March 2026, this collaboration expands from a single pilot project to deployment across more than 200 active projects, marking a notable shift in how the construction industry embraces advanced technology.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform portfolio-wide for progress monitoring, demonstrating a strong confidence in the technology's scalability across diverse project types. This extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue streams for Track3D, enhance customer loyalty, and establish a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In addition to the enterprise agreement, Track3D highlighted a successful case study at San Francisco International Airport. Hensel Phelps utilized Track3D's technology on this demanding build, where project leaders noted that the platform facilitated a shift from reactive to proactive decision-making. The deployment at SFO showcases how Track3D can enhance progress tracking and coordination across trades without necessitating significant changes to existing workflows.On the product development front, Track3D has announced a March release aimed at improving enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. New features include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which allow for centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, enhancements such as capture-free progress tracking capabilities enable teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, marking a significant improvement in operational flexibility for enterprise users.Track3D is also advancing its go-to-market strategy by focusing on specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. This event is pivotal for drywall and ceiling professionals, and Track3D aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and enhance margin protection. Overall, the recent developments with Hensel Phelps, the SFO case study, and the March product upgrades indicate a period of meaningful commercial traction for Track3D, potentially solidifying its competitive position in the construction technology landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUANTA SERVICES [3 News That Matters]

+ QUANTA SERVICES EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH TRI-CITY GROUP ACQUISITION

Quanta Services, Inc. has completed the acquisition of the Davenport-based Tri-City Group, a specialized contractor known for its expertise in electrical construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th April 2026, further solidifying Quanta's position as a leader in providing integrated engineering and construction services for electric power and renewable energy sectors.In a statement from Lori Rochholz, director of marketing for Tri-City Group, it was emphasized that the company will retain its name and brand identity post-acquisition. 'Tri-City Group will continue to operate as Tri-City Group. Our name is not changing. Our brand is not changing. The Palmer Family and the leadership team that has guided this company will continue to lead it,' she noted, assuring customers that they will experience the same quality and responsiveness as before, now enhanced by Quanta Services' extensive resources.Quanta Services, headquartered in Houston, Texas, also announced other acquisitions alongside Tri-City Group, including Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service. Duke Austin, president and CEO of Quanta Services, stated that these acquisitions broaden their craft-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs across the United States. With approximately 69,000 employees, Quanta Services is poised to leverage its expanded capabilities to meet growing customer demand in the infrastructure sector.The Tri-City Group, which rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diverse range of services, has a long-standing presence in the Quad Cities since 1895. It encompasses various subsidiaries, including Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, and has contributed to projects across 46 states and several countries. The company remains committed to investing in its local community, as highlighted in their recent statement, reaffirming their dedication to the region and its workforce.This acquisition not only enhances Quanta Services' operational footprint but also signifies a strategic move towards reinforcing its leadership in the electrical infrastructure market. As the company continues to expand its portfolio, the integration of Tri-City Group is expected to bring significant synergies, solidifying its competitive edge in the industry.

+ QUANTA SERVICES PROJECTS STRONG GROWTH AT JEFFERIES CONFERENCE

Quanta Services Inc. (NYSE:PWR) presented its strategic vision for 2026 during the Jefferies Power, Utilities, & Clean Energy Conference. On 3rd March 2026, the company shared insights into its optimistic outlook following a robust performance in the previous year. The management emphasized the importance of adapting to evolving market conditions while addressing both opportunities and challenges that lie ahead.In the session, Quanta's management expressed confidence in the growth potential of its business segments, particularly in the context of increasing demand for clean energy and infrastructure development. The company has positioned itself to capitalize on these trends, with plans to enhance its operational capabilities and expand its service offerings. The leadership team articulated a commitment to delivering quality results while maintaining accountability to their goals.The discussion also included Quanta's evolving strategy in the data center market, highlighting recent partnerships that reflect a careful approach to risk management. The executives noted that engaging with midcap utilities, such as NIPSCO, allows Quanta to offer tailored solutions that address specific needs while mitigating counterparty risks. This strategy is seen as a pivotal component of their growth plan moving forward.Furthermore, Quanta Services underscored its commitment to organic growth through careful capital allocation and strategic acquisitions. The management team conveyed that their focus extends beyond immediate gains, aiming for sustainable, long-term value creation. By maintaining a disciplined approach to investment, Quanta believes it can effectively navigate the complexities of the market while enhancing its competitive position.Overall, the insights shared at the Jefferies Conference reflect Quanta Services' proactive stance in a rapidly changing industry landscape. As the company gears up for 2026, it remains focused on leveraging its strengths to meet the demands of a growing market, ensuring it continues to deliver value to its stakeholders.

+ QUANTA SERVICES INC. POSITIONS ITSELF AT THE HEART OF NORTH AMERICA'S ENERGY TRANSITION

Quanta Services Inc. has become the go-to infrastructure platform for utilities, renewables, and grid modernization. On the 31st of December 2025, Quanta Services Inc. will be recognized as a critical execution engine in the North American energy transition, specializing in transforming essential infrastructure that underpins modern economies. While not a consumer-facing brand, its influence is felt across power lines, substations, pipelines, and fiber networks, crucial for supporting an increasingly electrified future.Amid rising demands for resilient energy systems, Quanta Services Inc. stands out by providing comprehensive solutions that address the challenges of upgrading outdated 20th-century networks. The company focuses on integrating engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services, which allows utilities to outsource entire projects, reducing the complexity of managing multiple contractors. This streamlined approach positions Quanta Services Inc. as a strategic partner for utilities facing pressures to adapt to climate resilience and accelerated decarbonization.Quanta Services Inc. operates through three core business lines: electric power infrastructure, underground utility and energy infrastructure, and communications infrastructure. This integrated platform allows Quanta to tackle a variety of projects, from high-voltage transmission lines that connect renewable energy sources to urban undergrounding and advanced communications networks. By leveraging cutting-edge technologies such as LiDAR and grid digitalization, Quanta Services Inc. enhances operational efficiency and risk management for its utility clients.In a competitive landscape with firms like MasTec Inc. and MYR Group Inc. vying for the same market segments, Quanta Services Inc. differentiates itself through its specialization and scale. While other companies may offer similar services, Quanta's focused approach on utility and network infrastructure enables it to execute large-scale projects more effectively. This specialization not only enhances its reputation as a reliable partner but also aligns with the long-term structural trends of the energy transition, making it a compelling option for utilities looking to invest in modernization efforts.For investors tracking Quanta Services Aktie (ISIN: US74762E1029), the company represents a unique opportunity to capitalize on the growing demand for robust energy infrastructure. With a premium valuation compared to traditional engineering peers, market sentiment reflects confidence in Quanta's ability to deliver sustainable growth amidst an evolving regulatory landscape. As utilities announce significant investments in grid hardening and renewable integration, Quanta Services Inc. is strategically positioned to capture a substantial portion of this expanding market, reinforcing its role as an indispensable partner in the energy transition.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ BARTON MALOW TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION OF ALUMNIFI FIELD FOR DETROIT CITY FC

Southfield-based Barton Malow has been selected as the construction manager for the Detroit City Football Club’s (Detroit City FC) new home stadium, AlumniFi Field. This announcement comes as the project is set to transform the site of the former Southwest Detroit Hospital into a 15,000-seat venue, with a projected opening date in spring 2027.The construction manager role was confirmed on 28th April 2026, as Detroit City FC continues to expand its presence in the USL Championship. With fan attendance at its current 7,000-seat home, Keyworth Stadium, regularly exceeding capacity, the demand for a larger facility has never been more pressing. AlumniFi Field will be strategically located between Detroit’s Corktown and Mexicantown neighborhoods, aiming to create a vibrant community hub.Designed to capture the essence of traditional European football stadiums, AlumniFi Field will feature a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands equipped with canopies, and rooms for various community activities. The venue is intended to host not only soccer matches but also concerts and other events, reinforcing its role as a gathering place for local residents.In collaboration with Detroit City FC, Barton Malow plans to honor the legacy of the former hospital, which was recognized for its commitment to diversity in hiring practices when it opened in 1973. Sean Hollister, senior vice president of Barton Malow, emphasized that this project aligns with the company's mission of technical excellence and community commitment.Sean Mann, CEO of Detroit City FC, echoed this sentiment, stating that AlumniFi Field will reflect the character and authenticity of Detroit. Both leaders expressed a shared vision for the stadium as a space that fosters community engagement and is shaped by the passions of local supporters.

+ BARTON MALOW TO LEAD WEST TOWER PROJECT AT MILAN PUSKAR STADIUM

MORGANTOWN, W.Va. - West Virginia University's Department of Intercollegiate Athletics will partner with Barton Malow and March-Westin to serve as construction managers on the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project, which is set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture brings together a power team of national and local construction leadership to the project.As the construction managers, Barton Malow and March-Westin will oversee all construction from conception to completion, ensuring that the project is finished safely, on time and within budget. By managing the daily on-site operations, the team will be the primary liaison between the university, architects and engineers, handling preconstruction planning, cost and schedule management, procurement, and coordination of onsite construction activities."We have experienced how special game day is at Milan Puskar Stadium, and we are excited to be a part of this partnership with the university on this renovation," said Scott Mull, Regional Executive at Barton Malow. "Our team is ready to bring the same focus and craftsmanship we've delivered on athletic facilities across the country to help create something Mountaineer fans will be proud of for generations."The joint venture combines complementary expertise in the unique campus and stadium environments. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. + MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

value.Title: kWh Analytics Reveals Top Risk Management Challenges for Renewable Energy Generation and Battery Energy Storage Systems Annual Solar Risk Assessment underscores critical importance of industry collaboration as grid dependence on renewables intensifies the need for resilience strategies

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-4VS3-S1S1-027T-00000-00kWh Analytics Reveals Top Risk Management Challenges for Renewable Energy Generation and Battery Energy Storage Systems2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexiskWh Analytics Reveals Top Risk Management Challenges for Renewable Energy Generation and Battery Energy Storage SystemsAnnual Solar Risk Assessment underscores critical importance of industry collaboration as grid dependence on renewables intensifies the need for resilience strategiesSAN FRANCISCO

kWh Analytics, a leader in underwriting the energy transition through its licensed insurance subsidiary, Solar Energy Insurance Services, today released its 8th annual Solar Risk Assessment (SRA), a comprehensive report designed to provide an objective, data-driven evaluation of renewable energy and battery energy storage systems (BESS) risk. The annual report includes contributions from academia, technology, financing, and insurance leaders in the solar and wind energy and BESS industries.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260512932725/en/

The Solar Risk Assessment is the leading publication on risk management and mitigation for renewable energy.

This year's report arrives at an inflection point for the energy transition, as unprecedented demand drives increasing reliance on solar, wind, and storage. At the same time, renewable energy and battery storage systems face ongoing operational and safety concerns, heightened cybersecurity threats, and intensified climate impacts amid a volatile regulatory landscape. Overcoming these challenges to meet demand will require robust collaboration between asset owners, operators, financiers, insurers, brokers, and manufacturers.

"Delivering durable, reliable, affordable renewable energy infrastructure requires the honest, data-driven exchange this report is built on," said kWh Analytics CEO, Jason Kaminsky. "The valuable research from this year's SRA contributors reflects the rigorous thinking our industry needs to build robust infrastructure in a heightened risk environment. We are grateful for their meaningful collaboration."

The 2026 SRA consists of 19 articles written by U.S. and global industry partners and provides an objective analysis of the top resilience, reliability, and emerging risks facing the renewable energy sector.

Key takeaways include:

While hail is still the most expensive type of insured loss, the industry is starting to turn its attention to fire risk. 84% of PV fire events are equipment-driven brushfires, not wildfires, meaning the risk originates within the plant itself.

We are identifying ever more nuanced operational risks, which are now measurable drags on revenue and useful life. Tracker twist, fungal soiling, junction box fires, and substantial anomalies are cropping up as unexpected yet impactful issues.

Financing and compliance terms are tightening. Only 38% of developers feel fully prepared for the new prohibited foreign entity (PFE) rules taking effect in 2026, and non-compliance with heightened regulatory standards can trigger penalties of $1M per day for renewable energy developers.

Findings in the 2026 SRA by category include:

Resilience

It's Not All About Stow: Hail-Hardened Modules Plus Robust Stow is Needed to Keep a 100MW Powerplant Below an Acceptable Loss Threshold for 13% of the US (kWh Analytics & Groundwork Renewables)

IEC Standards Underrepresent Cyclical Loading Experienced During a Real-World Hurricane by 8x (GameChange Solar)

32% More U.S. Wind Turbines Hit by 4+ Lightning Strokes in 2025 (Vaisala Xweather)

Big Losses, Limited View: Only 4% of PV Fire Loss Events Occur in High Wildfire Risk Areas, While 84% of Fire Events Are Due to Equipment-Driven Brushfire (kWh Analytics)

Invisible Ignition Risk: 79% of High-Risk PV Connector Failures Show No Thermal Signature at Time of Inspection (Nextpower)

30% of Manufacturers Exhibit Junction Box Failures in PQP Testing, Raising Fire Risk Across PV Portfolios (Kiwa PVEL & Kiwa PI Berlin)

Reliability

Choose Wisely: Assumed Useful Lives of 30 Years+ Make Project Financial Returns Increasingly Vulnerable to Module (kWh Analytics & DNV)

The Propeller Effect: How Tracker Operational Twist Can Reduce Total Yield by Over 2% and Increase Module Failure Risk (Azimuth Advisory Services)

Thermal Anomaly Data From Over 3,000 Assets Shows Degradation Accelerating After Year 7 (Above Surveying)

Blown Away: Industry Standard Practice of Modeling With Hourly Wind Data Underpredicts Energy Losses, Due to Tracker Wind Stow, by Up to 4% (Array Technologies)

Undersized Fuses Caused 15% Power Loss and Over $200k Annual Revenue Loss: A Case Study (Univers & Lightsoure bp)

Inverter Shutdowns Drive 28% of Recoverable Solar Performance Risk: Insights From 6.5 GW of Global Assets (SmartHelio)

In 2025, 34.2% of Substations Inspected > 1 Time Had a High Priority Anomaly (Raptor Maps)

Widespread Fungal Soiling Causing 5% Loss in the Humid Subtropical U.S. Alone (Solar Unsoiled)

Thermal Anomalies in BESS: 75% of Sites Show Early HVAC-Related Risk Signals (PowerUp)

SOC Inaccuracies Can Cost BESS Operators More Than $1 Million per GWh Annually (ACCURE Battery Intelligence)

Emerging Risks

New Prohibited Foreign Entity (PFE) Rules Take Effect in 2026. Only 38% of Developers Feel Fully Prepared (Crux)

Valuation "Step-Up" Risk Is a Binding Constraint for Renewable Energy Tax Insurance: 75% of Underwriters Won't Cover Above 25% (CAC)

1 Million Dollars a Day in FERC Penalties Can Be Avoided With Proper Cyber Security (Vaisala)

To access the complete 2026 Solar Risk Assessment, please visit kwhanalytics.com/industry-reports/2026-solar-risk-assessment/

About kWh Analytics

Solar Energy Insurance Services, Inc., a kWh Analytics company, a leader in insuring the energy transition, underwrites property insurance and revenue firming products for renewable energy assets. Our proprietary database of 300,000+ zero-carbon projects and $150B in loss data supports advanced modeling, insights, and precise risk assessment for renewable energy, mixed energy, and low-carbon projects. This data-driven approach incorporates resiliency measures in risk evaluation, promoting sustainable practices in the energy sector.

Our tailored solutions further our mission to underwrite the Energy Transition. Trusted by 15 global reinsurers and recognized by Insurance ERM Climate and Sustainability Awards as Sustainable Insurer of the Year, kWh Analytics continues to pioneer in the renewable energy insurance sector.

Learn more at https://www.kwhanalytics.com/ , or LinkedIn .

value.Title: Constellation Energy Corporation Q1 2026 Segmented Revenue Analysis *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S8GP-00R0-00000-00Constellation Energy Corporation Q1 2026 Segmented Revenue Analysis *Updated*2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisConstellation Energy Corporation Q1 2026 Segmented Revenue Analysis *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

This article includes relevant portions of a research report that we wrote and published analyzing Constellation Energy Corporation's Q1 2026 10-Q filing, which was made available following the company's earnings release on May 11, 2026.

INDEX

SECTION 1 SECTION 1 Analysis of Revenue by Segment and Geography

SECTION 2 Financials as reported (Quarter ending 31 March 2026)

SECTION 1 SECTION 1 Analysis of Revenue by Segment and Geography

Changes in Tone

- Management's tone across the reporting period reflects strong, quantified confidence in the consolidated corporate position to satisfy domestic aggregate demand for reliable, clean power generation, explicitly citing the enhanced operational strength of the combined fleet and integrated customer solutions stemming directly from the January 2026 corporate acquisition.

- The formalized commentary provided by the president and chief executive officer emphasizes tactical execution as the immediate organizational priority, specifically highlighting the necessity of operating legacy assets at a high utilization level, successfully integrating the operations of two large-scale entities, bringing newly developed generation resources to the commercial market, and systematically navigating an evolving state and federal regulatory environment.

- Statements issued by the executive vice president and chief financial officer convey a data-driven assertion of continued operational excellence across the entire expanded organization, framing the first-quarter financial and volumetric results as direct, empirical evidence of high-level performance and structural execution capabilities.

- The executive affirmation of the full-year 2026 Adjusted Operating Earnings guidance range of $11.00 to $12.00 per diluted share signals sustained management confidence in highly visible, recurring operating cash flows, which are anticipated to adequately support the corporation's established strategic capital allocation framework throughout the fiscal year.

- The operational narrative prominently highlights the successful commercial commissioning of the 105 megawatt Pastoria Solar Project, achieved on April 16, 2026, contextualizing this asset as the largest single renewable energy project contracted by the California Department of Water Resources to date, thereby validating the renewable development pipeline.

- Positive institutional recognition is allocated to the 460 megawatt Pin Oak Creek Energy Center, which successfully achieved commercial operation on April 30, 2026, a strategic infrastructure addition specifically designed to structurally strengthen grid reliability and dispatchable capacity within the high-demand ERCOT regional market.

- The corporate tone underscores external third-party validation of environmental, social, and governance initiatives through the receipt of the No. 1 ranking on Barron's annual Most Sustainable U.S. Companies list for 2026, a quantitative assessment based upon 230 distinct performance indicators encompassing environmental impact, workforce development metrics, and community support expenditures.

- Specific emphasis is placed on documenting internal workforce commitment and organizational culture, demonstrated quantitatively by employee participation in volunteering at over 150 formalized community events distributed across 15 states and WashingtonD.C. during April 2026, executed in honor of National Volunteer Month.

- Financial commentary systematically notes the favorable margin impacts derived from the strategic addition of Calpine, advantageous stock-based compensation variances, the realization of nuclear production tax credits, and broadly favorable market and portfolio pricing conditions that collectively drove Adjusted Operating Earnings higher, effectively offsetting the financial drag associated with unfavorable nuclear facility outages.

- The formalized introduction of the Equivalent Forced Outage Factor as a primary operational reporting metric for the newly expanded natural gas, oil, and pumped-storage hydro fleet, effective at the beginning of 2026, reflects a broadened, modernized view of consolidated fleet performance assessment post-acquisition.

- Operational performance of the legacy nuclear fleet is presented with explicit analytical focus, highlighting a 92.3 percent capacity factor for operated sites, while carefully noting the exclusion of the Salem and South Texas Project facilities from this specific metric, and simultaneously absorbing the impact of 99 planned refueling outage days during the measurement period.

- The overall forward-looking tone conveys calculated optimism regarding the expanded generation fleet's capacity to support localized economic expansion within the Texas market, substantiated by the formal Freestone net metering regulatory approval enabling the co-location of a commercial data center, alongside the execution of an exclusive contractual agreement for an additional 380 megawatt development phase.

Revenue by Segment

- Consolidated operating revenues expanded to $11,122 million during the first quarter of 2026, representing a substantial escalation from the $6,788 million recorded in the first quarter of 2025, primarily reflecting the immediate accretive financial impact of the comprehensively expanded generation fleet.

- Nuclear generation volumes provided a stabilizing baseload contribution to the segment portfolio, delivering 44,666 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, compared with 45,582 gigawatt-hours produced in the first quarter of 2025, reflecting scheduled operational sequencing.

- The natural gas, oil, and renewables generation segment recorded an acute volume expansion, reaching 32,061 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, accelerating sharply from the 5,905 gigawatt-hours documented in the first quarter of 2025, a growth vector driven entirely by the inclusion of Calpine operations.

- The strategic consolidation of Calpine operations beginning in January 2026 has significantly broadened the natural gas, oil, and renewables segment, augmenting the enterprise's capacity to support critical dispatchable power needs across variable market conditions.

- Wholesale purchased power activity maintained relative stability, standing at 16,603 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, measured against 16,935 gigawatt-hours transacted during the corresponding first quarter of 2025.

- Analyzed from an institutional investor perspective, the pronounced volume growth realized within the natural gas, oil, and renewables segment following the corporate acquisition structurally reduces revenue concentration risk tied strictly to nuclear output, while simultaneously enhancing overall portfolio dispatch flexibility and grid reliability for end customers.

- The recalibrated segment generation mix now optimally positions the corporate entity to capitalize on secular increases in baseline electricity demand spanning residential homes, commercial businesses, and heavy industry, with particular leverage in high-growth consumption areas.

SegmentThree Months Ended March 31, 2026Three Months Ended March 31, 2025Nuclear Generation44,66645,582Natural Gas Oil and Renewables32,0615,905Purchased Power16,60316,935

Revenue by Geography

- Aggregate total supply sales escalated significantly to 93,330 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, compared with the 68,422 gigawatt-hours reported in the first quarter of 2025, establishing a quantitative baseline that demonstrates overall platform scale expansion.

- Mid-Atlantic regional supply sales totaled 18,160 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, indicating slight moderation versus the 18,603 gigawatt-hours delivered in the first quarter of 2025.

- Within the Midwest market territory, supply sales amounted to 23,735 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, compared with 24,469 gigawatt-hours distributed during the first quarter of 2025.

- New York regional supply sales remained consistent, standing at 6,014 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, versus 6,280 gigawatt-hours recorded in the first quarter of 2025.

- Supply sales executed within the ERCOT marketplace were 5,776 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, measured against 6,272 gigawatt-hours delivered in the first quarter of 2025.

- Supply sales dispersed across Other Power Regions reached 11,059 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, compared with 12,798 gigawatt-hours distributed in the first quarter of 2025.

- The newly integrated Calpine supply sales asset base contributed a robust 28,586 gigawatt-hours to the consolidated portfolio in the first quarter of 2026, functioning as a new segment component with no prior-year comparable figure.

- The consolidated geographic distribution framework highlights the strategic and financial importance of the ERCOT regional market, where newly deployed resources like the Pin Oak Creek Energy Center directly address demographic commercial growth and structural grid reliability necessities.

- The organization's entrenched operational presence within the Mid-Atlantic and Midwest territories continues to serve as the anchor for stable, baseload nuclear output, while the integration of the Calpine asset base effectively enhances long-term geographic reach and expands dispatchable generation capacity.

- This geographic distribution profile provides institutional investors with diversified exposure to multi-nodal power markets, inherently mitigating regional regulatory risks through a balanced asset portfolio that optimally supports both zero-carbon clean energy mandates and baseline reliability demands.

Geographic AreaThree Months Ended March 31, 2026Three Months Ended March 31, 2025Mid-Atlantic18,16018,603Midwest23,73524,469New York6,0146,280ERCOT5,7766,272Other Power Regions11,05912,798Calpine28,586

Revenue by Product and Service Groups

- The consolidated product and service mix is strategically centered upon nuclear, natural gas, oil, renewables, and wholesale purchased power functioning as the primary supply sources for commercial operations.

- Nuclear generation assets reliably delivered 44,666 gigawatt-hours during the first quarter of 2026, directly supporting the enterprise's strategic market position as the nation's largest corporate producer of clean and highly reliable baseload energy.

- Natural gas, oil, and renewables generation, inclusive of the newly acquired Calpine asset contribution, reached a production volume of 32,061 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, up significantly from the baseline of 5,905 gigawatt-hours recorded in the first quarter of 2025.

- Wholesale purchased power activity was documented at 16,603 gigawatt-hours in the first quarter of 2026, maintaining operational parity compared with the 16,935 gigawatt-hours executed in the first quarter of 2025.

- The operational efficiency metric for renewable energy capture, specifically analyzing the wind, solar, and run-of-river hydro fleet, stood at 96.7 percent in the first quarter of 2026, representing an incremental margin improvement versus the 96.2 percent capture rate measured in the first quarter of 2025.

- Management utilized the Equivalent Forced Outage Factor metric to report a 4.5 percent rate for the expanded natural gas, oil, and pumped-storage hydro fleet during the first quarter of 2026, establishing a formalized baseline benchmarking metric for post-acquisition performance analysis.

- This evolution in the overarching product mix underscores a deliberate strategic pivot toward a highly diversified and fully dispatchable generation portfolio, which currently maintains the operational capacity capable of powering the equivalent of 27 million residential homes while consistently delivering approximately 10 percent of the nation's total clean energy supply.

- Capital market participants and shareholders directly benefit from the synergistic combination of stable, high-visibility nuclear output paired with highly flexible natural gas and renewables capacity, a configuration that precisely aligns with growing commercial data center load and industrial demand profiles, as empirically evidenced by the Freestone co-location regulatory approval.

- The integration of complementary service offerings, most notably the competitive retail energy supply segment serving approximately 2.5 million individual customer accounts nationwide, acts to structurally reinforce the core generation portfolio, hedge merchant exposure, and materially enhance long-term corporate earnings visibility.

Market PlatformThree Months Ended March 31, 2026Three Months Ended March 31, 2025Nuclear Generation44,66645,582Natural Gas Oil and Renewables32,0615,905Purchased Power16,60316,935

A year ago the Constellation Energy share price was $283.84. $1,000 would have bought 3.5 Constellation Energy shares on that day. Those 3.5 Shares would be worth $1,034 at today's share price of $293.59. PV$1000= $1,034.

urn:contentItem:6JJN-SVX3-S0MR-H3WX-00000-00Enphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:ENPH) Signs $52 Million Safe Harbor Agreement to Expand Domestic IQ9 Inverter Production2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisEnphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:ENPH) Signs $52 Million Safe Harbor Agreement to Expand Domestic IQ9 Inverter Production

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

11 May 2026 10:05 EDT

On May 7, 2026, Enphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:ENPH) signed a $52 million safe harbor agreement with a US solar and battery financing company focused on third party ownership models, aimed at strengthening domestic manufacturing and project execution. The deal commits IQ9 Microinverters manufactured in US facilities to upcoming residential and commercial installations, supporting eligibility for the base investment tax credit and the domestic content bonus credit. The company reports that its physical work test order backlog with TPO providers now stands at about $873.7 million, up from $754 million earlier in 2026. Revenue from this contract is expected to be recognized between 2027 and 2030, depending on customer project timelines and market demand. The current backlog consists entirely of IQ9 Microinverters, with potential for additional revenue from the future integration of IQ Batteries, cables and other accessories. Enphase Energy designs and manufactures microinverter based solar and battery storage systems for residential and commercial use, benefiting from the shift toward domestic manufacturing. The broader market environment includes ongoing tariffs and a trend toward onshoring, which some analysts believe could influence opportunities beyond this deal

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 ENPHASE ENERGY ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Enphase Energy Activities

Activities

Enphase Energy Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company headquartered in Fremont, California, is a leading innovator in the renewable energy sector. The company specializes in designing, developing, and manufacturing advanced microinverter systems, solar energy storage solutions, and intelligent software platforms that optimize energy production and consumption. At its core, Enphase focuses on enhancing the efficiency and reliability of solar power systems by converting direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) at the module level, reducing risks associated with traditional string inverters. The fastest-growing segments include its IQ-series microinverters and Encharge battery storage systems, which have seen rapid adoption in residential and commercial markets due to increasing demand for grid-independent energy solutions and supportive renewable policies. This growth is fueled by technological advancements, such as integrated app-based monitoring and seamless compatibility with emerging smart home ecosystems, positioning Enphase as a key driver in the global transition to sustainable energy. It is the NASDAQ's 8th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.
